summary: Discussions from Bluesky, HN, Reddit, and X highlight a major xAI/Grok Build open-sourcing privacy incident, Claude Code permission bypass fix, and user reports on agent workflow pain points (latency, database security, model selection, agent harness lock-in). Direction-changing signals include Grok Build privacy scandal, OpenAI Codex encryption controversy, and Microsoft Copilot OS leak. Gaps noted in mainstream platform changelogs and concrete user workflow reports. | Screening of official vendor sources: key releases from Anthropic, Mistral, Google, Vercel, Cloudflare, Daytona, Modal. Missing user_workflow and social/discussion sources. | Major releases from Google Gemini CLI, browser-use, and GitHub Copilot usage metrics dominate. New sandbox and eval tooling from UiPath, mitos-run, and AgentEval. Cline SDK/CLI updates and multiple agent framework releases (OpenHands, OpenCode, Goose). | Package registry signals: OpenAI Codex CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI, Vercel eval tooling, a new agent SDK (YA), memory innovations (AgenticOW, AxmeAI), MCP ecosystem expansion (Notion, Terraform), sandboxing (gigacode), and agent detection libraries.
